extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ Richard attacked final 300m on 4% uphill finish after peloton-splitting crash, won stage and gained crucial GC time
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma protected Roglic with only Kuss/Tolhoek/Hofland early, revealing thin mountain support compared to expectations at 95km out.
Three-man break (Frapporti/Timur/unnamed) gained 9min after sprint teams stopped chasing, exposing Lotto/Groupama's tactical capitulation mid-stage.
Late-stage crash split peloton into 8-rider group with mixed sprinters/GC riders; Carapaz attacked 300m out on 4% gradient.
UAE isolated to one man in final 1.4km despite protecting Roglic, showing domestique attrition issue on 400km+ racing.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: opportunistically attacked in chaos of crash-reduced group on uphill finish, showing both positioning awareness and punch
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: survived crash in front group but Jumbo protection collapsed to three riders by 95km mark
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: crashed and lost 4min05sec, described as 'injured warrior' who made 'real mess of himself'
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: spat out back of crash-reduced group before final kilometer despite being Italian champion
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: limited damage despite being behind main crash split, suggesting strong chase positioning
rider_signals.ACKERMAN Pascal: neutral: survived to final 8-rider group but positioned too far back to contest Carapaz attack
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: neutral: UAE led him out to second wheel but couldn't hold Carapaz's late acceleration
